entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A legume crop whose haulms were used as fodder for sheep

entities.name: SHEEP
entities.description: Growing male livestock fed with groundnut haulms

entities.name: DIGESTIBILITY
entities.description: The extent to which haulms are digested by sheep, measured in vivo and in vitro

entities.name: INTAKE
entities.description: The amount of groundnut haulms consumed by sheep daily

entities.name: NITROGEN RETENTION
entities.description: The amount of nitrogen retained by sheep after feeding on groundnut haulms

entities.name: LIVE WEIGHT GAIN
entities.description: The increase in body weight of sheep fed on groundnut haulms

entities.name: LIVESTOCK PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The overall performance and growth of sheep based on feeding systems

entities.name: ACID DETERGENT LIGNIN
entities.description: A measure of fodder quality in groundnut haulms that affects digestibility and growth

entities.name: IN VITRO METABOLISABLE ENERGY
entities.description: The energy content of groundnut haulms measured in vitro

entities.name: STEPWISE MULTIPLE REGRESSION
entities.description: A statistical procedure used to analyze the variation in digestibility and weight gain

entities.name: ICGV 89104
entities.description: A specific genotype of groundnut that promotes high livestock productivity

entities.name: ICGV 91114
entities.description: A specific genotype of groundnut that promotes high livestock productivity

entities.name: TMV 2
entities.description: A specific genotype of groundnut that promotes high livestock productivity

entities.name: ICGV 92093
entities.description: A specific genotype of groundnut that promotes high livestock productivity

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: A chemical element in groundnut haulms that affects nitrogen retention in sheep

relationships.description: Haulms from groundnut genotypes were fed to sheep
Groundnut haulms were tested for digestibility
Groundnut haulms were tested for intake levels
Groundnut genotypes affect nitrogen retention in sheep
Groundnut genotypes affect live weight gains in sheep
Choice of groundnut genotype significantly effects livestock productivity
ADL accounts for 58% of the variation in in vivo digestibility
ADL accounts for 39% of the variation in nitrogen retention
ADL accounts for 72% of the variation in live weight gains
Used to determine factors accounting for variation in in vivo digestibility
Used to determine factors accounting for variation in live weight gain
Combined with ADL to account for 92% of variation in live weight gain
Combined with ADL to account for 80% of variation in nitrogen retention
